name: Ductus arteriosus flow share
tier_rationale: Tier C from Rudolph 1985 lamb model. Sutton 1991 Heart (PMID 8142191) Doppler measurements in human fetuses confirm lung flow at ~22% (SD 7%) of combined ventricular output, leaving the bulk of right-ventricular output routed via ductus arteriosus. Human cohort data closes the gap from Rudolph's lamb extrapolation.

applicability.population: Late-gestation fetal lamb (extrapolated to human fetus)

notes: Fraction of right-ventricular output that bypasses the lungs via the ductus arteriosus to the descending aorta.

name: Foramen ovale streamline preference
tier_rationale: Tier C from Rudolph 1985 (lamb model) corroborated by Sutton 1991 Heart (PMID 8142191), which used Doppler echocardiography in n=38 human fetuses 18-37 weeks to measure foramen-ovale flow as 17-31% of combined ventricular output, exponentially rising threefold with gestational age. Human cohort directly informs Rudolph's lamb-extrapolated streamlining values.

applicability.population: Late-gestation fetal lamb (extrapolated to human fetus)

notes: Weight on the umbilical-vein pole in the cerebral weighted-average mix. 0.5 corresponds to no preferential streaming; Rudolph reports ~75-80% of the most-oxygenated DV/IVC stream crossing the foramen ovale to the left atrium. Caveats: Direct human in-vivo measurements are limited; Rudolph's lamb data is the standard reference.

name: Systemic venous return PO2
tier_rationale: Tier C from Kiserud 2001 Seminars in Perinatology (PMID 11254155): the ductus venosus shunts ~30% of umbilical-vein blood (well-oxygenated, ~30 mmHg PO2 in the human fetus) directly to the foramen ovale, bypassing the liver. PO2 of systemic-venous return is the mixed value after liver perfusion + DV bypass + return from lower body.
notes: Effective PO2 of systemic venous return mixing into the right atrium of the fetal heart. Caveats: Estimated from ductus venosus Doppler studies; tier C reflects indirect measurement.

name: Ductus-venosus shunt fraction in mid-pregnancy
tier_rationale: Tier B verified directly against Kiserud 2001 Seminars in Perinatology (PMID 11254155) abstract: 'The mean fraction of umbilical blood shunted through the ductus is reduced from 30% to 20% during the second half of the human pregnancy'. The 30% figure corresponds to the mid-pregnancy starting point.

extraction.method: Kiserud 2001 abstract directly states the 30% figure

notes: DV shunt fraction declines through gestation as the liver claims a larger share of umbilical-venous return.

name: Ductus-venosus shunt fraction in late pregnancy
tier_rationale: Tier B verified directly against Kiserud 2001 (PMID 11254155) abstract: 30% → 20% reduction across the second half of pregnancy. The 20% figure is the late-pregnancy/near-term endpoint. The declining shunt indicates 'the fetal liver has a higher priority' through the third trimester.

extraction.method: Kiserud 2001 abstract directly states the 20% figure

notes: The DV can re-dilate under hypoxic stress, shunting more flow back through to bypass the liver — a key autoregulatory response.

name: Fetal heart rate, first-trimester baseline
tier_rationale: Tier B from Pildner von Steinburg 2013 (PMID 23740338) longitudinal Doppler cohort: FHR peaks ~170 bpm around weeks 9-10, then declines as parasympathetic tone develops.

name: Umbilical artery pulsatility index, mid-second-trimester baseline
tier_rationale: Tier A from Acharya 2005 (PMID 15776417) reference ranges: UA-PI ~1.5 at 16-20 weeks, smoothly falls to ~0.8 by term as placental vascular resistance drops.

name: Umbilical artery pulsatility index at term
tier_rationale: Tier A from Acharya 2005: term UA-PI ~0.8-0.9. Elevated UA-PI at term flags placental insufficiency / FGR.

name: Middle cerebral artery pulsatility index, early second trimester
tier_rationale: Tier B from Mari 1995 (PMID 7900181) longitudinal Doppler reference: MCA-PI ~1.5 at ~20 weeks, rises through T2, peaks ~28 weeks at ~2.0, then falls as fetal cerebral vasodilation drops resistance near term.

name: Middle cerebral artery pulsatility index, mid-pregnancy peak
tier_rationale: Tier B from Mari 1995: MCA-PI peak ~2.0 at ~28 weeks. The bell-shaped trajectory falls toward ~1.5 at term as the fetal brain claims more flow under late-pregnancy hypoxic stress (brain-sparing reflex).

name: Fetal heart rate at term
tier_rationale: Tier A: normal-range FHR at term is 110-160 bpm (NICHD definitions), with the modal value ~140 bpm. Pildner von Steinburg 2013 longitudinal data show the smooth decline from the T1 peak through term.
